title: Natalie Baclawski
text:
Hello! My name is Natalie Baclawski, and welcome to my portfolio website. This site highlights my academic background, technical projects, and professional interests in data analytics, machine learning, and business problem-solving.
I created this portfolio to showcase the work I have completed in graduate school and to provide a professional overview of my skills, experience, and career goals.


I am currently a graduate student at BU’s MET College, pursuing a Masters in Business Analytics while working in the finance industry.




Data Analysis
Python
SQL
Excel Modeling
Financial Analysis




Here are some projects I am/have worked on:

Boston Property Value Analysis
Northwind SQL Database Analysis
Time Series Forecasting in R

title: Projects
text:
This project explored salary differences across AI and non-AI roles using large-scale job posting data. I cleaned and analyzed the dataset using Python, Spark, and Quarto, then created visualizations to examine salary patterns by industry, region, and work arrangement.
Tools used: Python, PySpark, Quarto, Plotly, Pandas

title: Projects
text:
New York Real Estate Analysis
This project analyzed New York City real estate data to identify pricing trends, neighborhood-level differences, and key drivers of property value. The analysis combined SQL-based data extraction, Python for data cleaning and feature engineering, and Power BI for interactive visualization. Key insights included variations in pricing across boroughs, the impact of property characteristics on valuation, and patterns in market demand.
Tools used: Python, SQL, Power BI

title: About Me
text:
About Me
I am a graduate student in Applied Data Analytics with interests in data-driven decision-making, machine learning, business analytics, and data visualization. My academic work has focused on analyzing job market trends, building predictive models, and presenting findings through clear visualizations and professional reports.
My technical experience includes Python, SQL, Spark, AWS, machine learning, and data visualization tools. I am especially interested in applying analytics to real-world business problems and building projects that connect technical skills with strategic insight.
My career goal is to work in a role where I can combine analytical thinking, technical tools, and business communication to support data-informed decisions. I currently work at a financial institution, as a financial analyst.
